title: Integrated Operating-System, Application, Mobile, and Security Triage · Support Workbench
summary: A guided support bench extends integrated software and security fault tree while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of integrated software and security fault tree while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate performance, resource, compatibility, corruption, configuration, update, account, policy, storage, network, hardware, malicious, and user-workflow hypotheses.
Correlate reproduction, process, service, dependency, logs, resource, update, driver, disk, integrity, safe mode, clean boot, profile, and changed-user evidence.
Trace identity, lockout, time, certificate chain, hostname, trust, proxy, captive portal, browser profile, extension, permissions, redirect, and network interception.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Slow startup, crashes, redirects, lockouts, certificate warnings, mobile pop-ups, and network errors follow an unknown change. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate performance, resource, compatibility, corruption, configuration, update, account, policy, storage, network, hardware, malicious, and user-workflow hypotheses. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope, keep Software Scope connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Slow startup, crashes, redirects, lockouts, certificate warnings, mobile pop-ups, and network errors follow an unknown change. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Software Scope condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 2 220-1202 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 2 operating-system, security, software-troubleshooting, and operational-procedure objective and weighting.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Software Scope precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Slow startup, crashes, redirects, lockouts, certificate warnings, mobile pop-ups, and network errors follow an unknown change. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Software Scope, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A computer that becomes slow after an update proves the update is defective.

options.text: State the Software Scope preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A computer that becomes slow after an update proves the update is defective. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Software Scope.

For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Software Scope for integrated software and security fault tree.

stimulus.title: Software Security Proof case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Slow startup, crashes, redirects, lockouts, certificate warnings, mobile pop-ups, and network errors follow an unknown change. The workshop variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. lines.label: risk
lines.text: If the user can resume work, security and recovery checks can be deferred indefinitely.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Repair the first supported cause, verify original and changed identities, applications, networks, restarts, updates, protections, backups, accessibility, and recurrence monitoring. Artifact under review: integrated software and security fault tree.

options.text: The Software Security Proof record says integrated software and security fault tree looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the initial evidence packet evidence boundary for Software Security Proof.
